title: CSS Selectors, Tree Navigation, Cardinality, Text, and Attributes · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers css selectors, tree navigation, cardinality, text, and attributes into a changed selector and extraction contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of selector and extraction contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Use type, class, ID, attribute, combinator, grouping, and supported pseudo-class selectors with explicit scope and library support.
State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
Anchor extraction at a stable semantic component and resolve fields relative to it instead of coupling to page-wide positions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Attribute Optional Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-attribute-optional-policy-1fwnzl" that uses case 7065 to reconcile selector scope, cardinality, relative fields, text policy, missingness, and node provenance. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Attribute Optional Policy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Attribute Optional Policy into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Attribute Optional Policy, keep Attribute Optional Policy connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-attribute-optional-policy-1fwnzl
# Competency: scraper-attribute-optional-policy.
# Case 7065: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_attribute_optional_policy_1fwnzl(component_nodes: int = 8, extracted_records: int = 8, ambiguous_matches: int = 0, missing_fields: int = 3, provenance_nodes: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1fwnzl = "1fwnzl-lznwf1-7065"
    assert evidence_variant_1fwnzl
    evidence_axes_1fwnzl = (
        "authorization1fwnzl",
        "url7065",
        "responselznwf1",
        "tree1fwnzl7065",
        "frontier70651fwnzl",
        "reportlznwf17065",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1fwnzl)) == 6
    if component_nodes < 1 or extracted_records < 0:
        return False, extracted_records, "selector-count"
    if extracted_records != component_nodes:
        return False, extracted_records, "record-cardinality"
    if ambiguous_matches > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_matches, "ambiguous-selector"
    if missing_fields < 0 or provenance_nodes != extracted_records:
        return False, provenance_nodes, "field-provenance"
    assert extracted_records == component_nodes and ambiguous_matches == 0
    return True, extracted_records, "provenance-selector-contract"
